specification.topic-statement: The PGresult lifecycle manager wraps a PostgreSQL query result in a talloc node so that it is automatically released when the enclosing talloc context is freed.

scope.in-scope: Talloc wrapper creation for PGresult pointers and automatic PQclear invocation via talloc destructor.
scope.boundary: Query execution and PGresult allocation are performed by the PostgreSQL client library. This topic covers only the talloc integration for automatic cleanup.

behavior.wrapper-creation:
A talloc node is allocated as a child of the provided context. The PostgreSQL result pointer is stored in the node. A destructor is registered on the node that will be invoked automatically when the node or any of its ancestors is freed.
If the talloc allocation fails, the process aborts. If a null result pointer is provided, the wrapper is created with a null pointer; the destructor handles this safely.
behavior.automatic-cleanup:
When the wrapper node is freed (directly or because an ancestor context is freed), the destructor calls PQclear on the stored result pointer, returning the PostgreSQL library's internal memory to the C allocator. The destructor then clears the stored pointer and returns zero to satisfy the talloc destructor protocol.
The destructor checks whether the stored pointer is null before calling PQclear, making it safe to create wrappers around null results without risk of a null pointer dereference.
